question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user poison the mirror node REST endpoint (cfg.mirrorRest via HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST env var) to redirect all NewTransferTransaction() submissions to an attacker-controlled node, causing every pinger-generated transfer to be recorded only in a forged ledger rather than the real Hashgraph, permanently corrupting the observable transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user intercept the unencrypted HTTP response from cfg.mirrorRest (no TLS enforcement in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes) and inject fake serviceEndpoint entries so that cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) submits signed transactions to rogue consensus nodes, causing those transactions to never reach Hashgraph consensus and creating a false history of successful transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that cfg.amountTinybar accepts negative values (config.go only rejects zero, not negatives) to craft a transfer where AddHbarTransfer(client.GetOperatorAccountID(), HbarFromTinybar(-cfg.amountTinybar)) becomes a credit and AddHbarTransfer(toID, HbarFromTinybar(cfg.amountTinybar)) becomes a debit, injecting a reversed-direction transaction into Hashgraph history that misrepresents fund flow?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to the hardcoded default key (302e020100300506032b65700422042091132178e72057a1d7528025956fe39b0b847f200ab59b2fdd367017f3087137 from config.go) to sign and submit arbitrary NewTransferTransaction() calls as the operator, injecting fabricated transfer records into Hashgraph history without any authorization check?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to point to an attacker-controlled account so that every periodic cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) call permanently records transfers to the attacker's account in Hashgraph history, creating a falsified audit trail of fund movements?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retry loop (for i := 1; i <= attempts; i++) by causing transient failures on legitimate nodes while keeping a rogue node available, forcing all cfg.maxRetries+1 attempts to eventually land on the attacker's node and recording a fraudulent transaction in a shadow ledger instead of the real Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a DNS rebinding attack against the cfg.mirrorRest hostname so that fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() initially resolves to a legitimate mirror node but subsequent SDK connections resolve to an attacker-controlled IP, causing cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) to submit transactions to a fake node and pollute Hashgraph history with unconfirmed transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of receipt.Status validation beyond a nil-error check (rerr == nil) to accept a forged receipt with an arbitrary status string from a rogue node, causing the pinger to log a false 'transfer success' and treat a non-existent Hashgraph transaction as confirmed history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malicious DomainName value (e.g., an internal Kubernetes service hostname) into the mirror node /api/v1/network/nodes JSON response so that net.JoinHostPort(host, port) in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() builds a network map pointing to internal infrastructure, causing cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) to submit transactions to unintended internal endpoints and bypass Hashgraph consensus?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger integer overflow in backoff() by setting HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to a large value (e.g., 63+), causing 1<<(attempt-1) to overflow to zero or negative, resulting in a zero-duration sleep that floods the Hashgraph network with rapid-fire duplicate transaction submissions and creates a history of replayed transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to a victim account ID (e.g., 0.0.98) so that AddHbarTransfer(client.GetOperatorAccountID(), HbarFromTinybar(-cfg.amountTinybar)) debits the victim's account in every Hashgraph transaction, creating a falsified history of unauthorized withdrawals from that account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of TLS certificate pinning in the http.Client{Timeout: cfg.mirrorNodeClientTimeout} used by fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to perform a man-in-the-middle attack, replacing legitimate node endpoints with attacker-controlled ones so that all subsequent Execute(client) calls submit transactions to fake nodes, erasing those transfers from real Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to the maximum int64 value so that HbarFromTinybar(-cfg.amountTinybar) overflows to a large positive number, causing NewTransferTransaction() to submit a transaction with an astronomically large debit that, if accepted by a node, permanently records a fraudulent large-value transfer in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that nodesEnvelope.Links.Next pagination is parsed but never followed in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork(), causing the pinger to build an incomplete network map that omits legitimate nodes and routes all Execute(client) calls exclusively through a small set of potentially compromised nodes, skewing Hashgraph transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an IPAddressV4 value containing a loopback address (127.0.0.1) or link-local address into the mirror node response so that net.JoinHostPort builds a network map pointing to localhost, causing Execute(client) to submit transactions to a local process rather than Hashgraph consensus nodes, fabricating false transaction receipts?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to an extremely small value (e.g., 1ns) to cause the ticker in main.go to fire at maximum rate, flooding Hashgraph with thousands of NewTransferTransaction() submissions per second and creating a massive volume of artificial transfer history that overwhelms the ledger's record integrity?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of transaction memo validation to inject arbitrary data into the Hashgraph ledger via the transfer transaction memo field (if exposed by the SDK), permanently embedding attacker-controlled data into immutable Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID equal to the operator account ID so that both AddHbarTransfer calls reference the same account, causing NewTransferTransaction() to submit a self-transfer that records a zero-net-effect transaction in Hashgraph history, polluting the ledger with meaningless entries that obscure legitimate activity?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that resp.GetReceipt(client) queries the receipt from the same potentially-compromised client network map, so that a rogue node returns a SUCCESS receipt for a transaction that was never submitted to real Hashgraph consensus, causing the pinger to permanently log a false transaction ID as confirmed history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user perform an SSRF attack by setting HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to an internal metadata service URL (e.g., http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/) so that buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() fetches cloud instance credentials, which are then used to sign and submit fraudulent transactions to Hashgraph under the operator's identity?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to an attacker-controlled server that returns a crafted nodesEnvelope JSON, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to build a client pointing entirely to attacker nodes, so that every Execute(client) call submits transactions to a shadow network that never records them in real Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response body size limits in json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&payload) to send a gigabyte-scale JSON response from a rogue mirror node, causing the pinger process to exhaust memory and crash before any legitimate transactions are submitted, creating a gap in Hashgraph transaction history that could be exploited to hide tampering?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a nodeEntry with a NodeAccountID matching a legitimate Hedera node (e.g., '0.0.3') but with attacker-controlled ServiceEndpoints, causing hiero.AccountIDFromString(n.NodeAccountID) to succeed and the SDK to associate a real node account ID with a fake endpoint, so that Execute(client) routes transactions to the attacker's server while the receipt appears to come from a legitimate node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to zero (bypassing the <=0 guard via a crafted duration string that parses to a very small positive value) so that backoff() returns near-zero sleep durations, causing the retry loop in submitWithRetry() to hammer Hashgraph nodes with rapid duplicate transaction attempts and create a history of replayed or conflicting transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the pinger logs resp.TransactionID.String() on success without verifying the transaction ID matches what was submitted, allowing a rogue node to return a receipt with a different transaction ID, causing the pinger to log a fabricated transaction ID as confirmed Hashgraph history?
